The city of Colorado Springs, Colorado has a funny yet repellant motto: “Keep Colorado Springs Lame.” The last few times I’ve visited, I must say it lived up to its reputation. I wasn’t particularly inspired by it, but a nagging feeling told me I wasn’t giving this town a fair shake. This time, I made a specific effort to seek out unique things to do in Colorado Springs, or within an hour of Colorado Springs. To my delight, I found that its “lameness” might just be a cover.

At first glance, some of my title headings may seem like obvious things to do, but I’ve pointed out the more unique or unexpected aspects of those destinations in the text.

## Paint Mines Interpretive Park

###### Visit Colorado Springs’ Awe-Inspiring National Natural Landmark

## Garden of the Gods Visitor & Nature Center

## Welcome to Garden of the Gods!

In 1909, Garden of the Gods Park was deeded to the City of Colorado Springs under the condition that this one-of-a-kind place remain “forever free” to the public. The innovative public-private partnership between the City of Colorado Springs Parks, Recreation and Cultural Services Department, Garden of the Gods Visitor & Nature Center, and Garden of the Gods Foundation help protect and preserve this Natural National Landmark for future generations to explore.

## National Natural Landmark

Garden of the Gods is a free City Park & National Natural Landmark, not a state or national park.### [Garden of the Gods Park Information](/park-info/)

## Your Visit Supports Our Mission

**Garden of the Gods Visitor and Nature Center Mission:**  
To provide public education and a continuous stream of revenue for Garden of the Gods Park.

**Garden of the Gods Foundation Mission:**  
To improve the quality of Garden of the Gods Park by gathering resources and providing grants that enhance and protect this registered National Natural Landmark.

*Thank you to our visitors, volunteers, and donors for joining our mission of being good stewards of Garden of the Gods Park. Every purchase and donation made at the Visitor Center, whether in the café or gift shop, participation in educational programs and tours, or by becoming a member of the Garden of the Gods Foundation, directly supports the Garden of the Gods Park!*"

When visitors ask about where the springs are in Colorado Springs, the answer is [Manitou Springs](/areas/manitou-springs/). Currently, there are 8 springs open to the public, each with its own distinctive flavor and effervescence. Centuries ago, the great tribes of the plains and the Mountain Utes paid homage to the healing and spiritual powers of the mineral springs and believed the medicinal waters were a gift from the Great Spirit Manitou.

## What are the Manitou Mineral Springs?

Rainwater and snow melt from [Pikes Peak](/things-to-do/pikes-peak/) and surrounding mountains soak into rock fractures. As the water penetrates to great depth, it becomes heated and mineralized. The warm water naturally flows up into cavernous limestone where it becomes carbonated, then issues from the numerous natural and drilled springs throughout Manitou. Because the water takes thousands of years to complete its journey from mountain ...[truncated 2299 chars]...*

Wheeler Spring, a drilled soda spring, was donated to the city by the family of Jerome Wheeler, of the New York Macy's, residing in Manitou Springs and involved in banking, mining and railroads locally and in Aspen. His home, Windemere, was located at the site of the present day post office.

[Download the Brochure & Walking Map](https://www.mineralspringsfoundation.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2020-MSF-Brochure.pdf)

## adventure in every season

A journey on the Cog Railway is full of scenic beauty all year round. While the weather and breathtaking views are varied depending on the season, you’ll always be able to find an updated schedule with available departure dates and times when exploring our [Purchase Tickets](/ride/purchase-tickets/) page!

 

## SPRing

Springtime in the Rockies is when wildflowers begin to pop up and wildlife comes out of a long hibernation. Lingering snow has almost melted and days are long as the sun begins to warm the earth.

 

## SUMMER

Longer days bring more opportunities to experience one of the prettiest mountain rides Colorado has to offer. With daily operations throughout the season, you get to choose between early morning dew or golden-hour on America’s Mountain.

 

## FALL

Fall is the time when days are the sunniest and the nights crisp and clear. The aspens begin to turn in late September through mid-October and are always a highlight.

 

## WINTER

Winter is the clearest time of year as the low humidity and snowfall decrease dust in the air. Although days are short, the bright sun can still be warm.

## THE JOURNEY

Each round trip aboard the Pikes Peak Cog Railway is approximately three breathtaking hours of sightseeing. Of course, you’ll need plenty of time to get situated, so we recommend arriving 30 minutes ahead of your departure and suggest stretching your experience to four hours to allow time to find that perfect souvenir at the gift shop.

  

## THE TRAINS

Our Cog Railway is serviced by three new trains that can carry up to 263 passengers per trip and four refurbished railcars that carry 214 passengers each."

Art on the Streets accepts proposals from November through mid-February.

[APPLICATION INFORMATION](https://www.downtowncs.com/art-on-the-streets-call-for-entries/)

Each year, 12 works are selected by an independent jury. The primary selection criteria is artistic merit. Secondary criteria include installation logistics, maintenance and safety concerns, and inclusion of a diverse range of media and perspectives. All works will be evaluated for stability by a structural engineer contracted by Downtown Ventures. While sculpture tends to dominate the entry pool, other types of art suitable for outdoor display are encouraged, including murals as well as interactive and multimedia works. Art on the Streets also has the ability to work with artists on larger and/or site-specific installations.

Michelle is fortunate to work with an “encyclopedic” collection of over 11,000 works and a broad mission to engage with contemporary artists around the world. Upscale New American cuisine and cocktails — waiting for you just beneath the Springs.

## Experience Colorado Springs’ nightlife underground.

Journey down the subway steps and experience a new kind of nightlife. Eclectic cuisine, exotic cocktails, haunting decor — It’s no wonder The Rabbit Hole has been nicknamed the Wonderland of the Springs.

With upscale New American cuisine and a Wonderland-inspired ambiance, The Rabbit Hole is the perfect place for late-night bites, end-of-shift cocktails, and that elusive “something different” you’ve been searching for all this time.

Enjoy one of our internationally and seasonally changing small plates under the haunting gaze of Phil Lear and Douglas Rouse’s artwork, and drink in the gothic atmosphere with friends and a flaming cocktail. Secure your reservation at one of Downtown’s best-kept secrets, and experience nightlife underground. Enjoy an eclectic fine dining experience seven days a week from 4 PM to 11 PM.

### Voted Best Of Silver – Best Overall Restaurant, Best Fine Dining

## A love story between the mountains and the desert.

At Four, the rugged peaks of Colorado meet the sun-soaked deserts of the Southwest. Our seasonal four-course tasting menu tells the story of this union, blending wild game, river fish, farm harvests, and foraged treasures into a cuisine rooted in the Four Corners.

Every plate is a reflection of balance—earth and fire, tradition and innovation, heritage and creativity. Guests are invited to explore this journey with hand-crafted cocktails, curated wines, and local craft beers designed to complement the flavors of the land.

Chef Brother Luck reimagines Southwestern dining as an intimate experience where landscapes, cultures, and flavors converge.

## cave tours

## Cave history

History books may say that Colorado’s Cave of the Winds was discovered in 1880, but the cave itself is millions of years old. Long before that, the Jicarilla Apaches told stories of a sacred cave near Manitou Springs—home to the Great Spirit of the Wind.

When two young brothers named George and John Pickett went exploring in the Williams Canyon area, they discovered the cave entrance, what we now know as Cave of the Winds.

###### Daily tours

## Discovery Tour

An Ideal Family Tour! Explore the electrically lit portion of Cave of the Winds on a 45 – 60 minute guided walking tour which includes 15 rooms, a half mile of concrete walkways, and 196 stairs (not all at once!). On this family-friendly tour with an expert guide, you will learn about the history and geology of the caves and see some of its most beautiful formations.

*Note:* *Due to low ceilings, narrow passages, and st...[truncated 10276 chars]...ion Tour: Ages 13+ only. Be sure to check age requirements when booking!

Got more questions?

[See our FAQs page](/plan-your-day/faqs/) for more useful information about your visit.

**Already booked your tour reservation and need to reschedule or cancel?** To cancel or reschedule your reservation you must do so at least **ONE HOUR** prior to your scheduled tour time. ALL tour tickets will be non-refundable after the tour time has passed. For more information see our [Terms & Conditions](/terms-conditions/#general). To reschedule or cancel [fill out a request online](/refund-reschedule-request/) or contact us at 719-685-5444 ext. 6.

###### Elevate your tour

## Add the Pioneer Package

Perfect for the whole family! Explore and soar when you choose this fun aboveground package. The Pioneer package includes one ride per person on each of the included attractions: the Wind Walker Challenge Course, Bat-A-Pult aerial adventure, Cliff Hanger Climbing Wall, and Geronimo’s Leap."
